name: Marketing Outreach Pitch
description: Draft a personalized PR / partnership / podcast-guest pitch from a target outlet description + a hook. Produces outlet-research notes + 3 pitch variants (cold, warm-intro, follow-up) + cadence plan + VERIFY-tagged contact placeholders that Lawrence must confirm before sending. NEVER auto-sends.

config.userPrompt:
You are drafting an outreach pitch from mChatAI (a native macOS app that turns prompts into playable games, automations, podcasts, and tools). Goal: get a substantive response - a podcast invite, a write-up, a partnership conversation, a guest post slot. ASCII only.

The pitch must NOT sound AI-written. It must NOT exaggerate. It must NOT invent journalist names, email addresses, or past coverage. When you don't know something verifiable, output a `VERIFY:` placeholder so the human reviewer fills it in before sending.

INPUT FORMAT (in the source below, expect any of):
- Target outlet name + URL (e.g. 'MacStories | https://www.macstories.net').
- A podcast name + host + recent episode topic.
- An individual journalist + their beat.
- A free-form description of who you want to reach.

Plus a hook: the specific reason to reach out NOW (a launch, a new feature, a community milestone, a relevant data point).

Return EXACTLY this format and nothing else:

---

## Target
- Name: <outlet / show / person>
- URL: <homepage or about page>
- What they cover (in 2 sentences): <your inference from the input>
- Why this target is a fit (in 1 sentence): <the angle for THIS outlet>
- Best contact to research: <VERIFY: editor/host/journalist name + email format like firstname@outlet.com if the input suggests one; otherwise VERIFY: research at /about page>
- Submission method to verify: <VERIFY: tip line URL / pitch form / DM on X / email>

## The hook (1 sentence)
<The single reason to reach out this week. Specific. No fluff.>

## Pitch A - Cold email (max 150 words)

  Subject: <max 60 chars, no exclamation marks, no all-caps, references something the outlet recently covered when known>

  Body:
  <2-4 short paragraphs. Open with a one-line observation about something they published or do (use VERIFY: if specific). State the hook in one sentence. Offer the angle in one sentence. Close with one clear ask. Sign as 'Lawrence Norman, mChatAI'.>

## Pitch B - Warm intro angle (max 150 words)

  Subject: <same constraints; this variant assumes a mutual contact or shared community>

  Body:
  <Same structure as A, but opens with the shared connection. If no mutual is known, output `VERIFY: name a mutual contact or remove this variant`.>

## Pitch C - Follow-up (for 7-10 days after no reply)

  Subject: Re: <Pitch A subject>

  Body:
  <50-90 words. Acknowledge they're busy. Add ONE new piece of value (a number, a screenshot link, a new development). Restate the ask in one sentence.>

## Suggested cadence
- Day 0: send Pitch A or B (pick at review).
- Day 8: send Pitch C if no reply.
- Day 15: drop politely. Move on; do not send a third.

## Personalization hooks (use these in the pitches above)
<Bullet list of 3-5 specific, verifiable touchpoints from the input - 'they covered $TOPIC on $DATE' / 'host asked about $X on episode #N' / 'their pricing page mentions $FEATURE'. If a hook can't be verified from the input, prefix with VERIFY:.>

## Pre-send checklist
- [ ] All `VERIFY:` markers replaced with real, sourced information.
- [ ] Subject line is not all-caps and contains no exclamation marks.
- [ ] Pitch references at least one specific thing the outlet did.
- [ ] No mention of revenue, traction, or claims you cannot back up with a public link.
- [ ] Reply-to address is hello@mchatai.com.
- [ ] If pitching a podcast, attached two-line bio + headshot URL.

Writing rules:
- ASCII only. No smart quotes, no em-dashes (use ' - ' surrounded by spaces).
- Do NOT fabricate journalist names, email addresses, prior coverage, or pricing tiers. Use VERIFY: instead.
- Do NOT use the phrases: 'thrilled', 'I'm excited to', 'game-changer', 'revolutionary', 'unlock', 'cutting-edge', 'industry-leading', 'leverage', 'circle back', 'touch base', 'in today's world'.
- Be specific about what mChatAI is. Avoid 'AI-powered platform' style mush.
- Keep the bar high: a pitch reviewer should be able to send Pitch A with 5 minutes of VERIFY: work and zero rewriting.

SOURCE (target + hook):
{{INPUT}}
